## Page 178 — Science Reasoning (Figure Shared by 3 Questions)

**Study the diagram below.**

[FIGURE: Bar graph showing average monthly rainfall (mm) for Brisbane, Sydney and Melbourne across 12 months. Brisbane peaks in January at 160mm. Sydney peaks in June at 130mm. Melbourne is relatively flat, peaking in October at 65mm.]

**Figure 1: Average Monthly Rainfall for Three Australian Cities**

**14.** In which month does Brisbane receive its highest average rainfall?

(A) October
(B) November
(C) December
(D) January

**15.** Which city shows the most consistent (least variable) monthly rainfall across the year?

(A) Brisbane
(B) Sydney
(C) Melbourne
(D) All three are equally consistent

**16.** Approximately how much more rainfall does Brisbane receive in January than Melbourne receives in its wettest month?

(A) 75 mm
(B) 95 mm
(C) 115 mm
(D) 160 mm

**17.** Which of the following conclusions is NOT supported by the figure?

(A) Brisbane has a summer-dominant rainfall pattern
(B) Sydney has a winter-dominant rainfall pattern
(C) Melbourne has an evenly distributed rainfall pattern
(D) Melbourne's annual rainfall total exceeds Sydney's
